violations.text: The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2025
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025

Various foods were observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of all foods out of temperature. Food has been stored from the morning or the day before. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025

The lowboy unit was observed unable to hold an adequate ambient temperature and was observed iced over. Unit was taken out of service and to not be used until serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap (currently has an air break). A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Per PIC, owners husband will install a proper air gap. Guidance document provided. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. Per PIC, class will be taken on site,. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

Chicken and guac was observed thawing on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Both products were still frozen. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap (currently has an air break). A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Per PIC, owners husband will install a proper air gap. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150-400 ppm. Establishment to manually add sanitizer to 3-bay sink and test using strips until sanitizer pump is serviced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Meatballs were observed held under 135 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Meatballs were reheated. Proper hot holding methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Currently prep sink has an air break. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024
The establishment does not have an allergen statement posted for consumer awareness. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the sanitizer bucket was 100 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200-400 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2023
The veggie prep sink was observed with an air-break. The food prep sinks must have an air-gap. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023
